description: default config
k=50 BatchTopK (higher k hurts precision, worse than k=25 baseline)
DiverseTopKSAE k=40 diversity_coeff=0.02 (marginal gain over baseline)
BatchTopK k=50 (same as agent3 finding, higher k hurts precision)
ISTABatchTopK 3 steps step_size=0.3 k=25 (iterative encoder refinement, +5.4% over baseline)
MatryoshkaBatchTopK widths=[256,512,1024,2048,4096] k=25 use_matryoshka_aux_loss=true (+14.5% over baseline, +9% over ISTA)
ISTAMatryoshka widths=[256,512,1024,2048,4096] k=25 ista_steps=3 step_size=0.3 (+29% over baseline, +13.8% over Matryoshka alone)
DeepEncoderMatryoshka 2-layer MLP encoder + Matryoshka (WORSE: deep encoder hurt alignment, MCC=0.49)
ISTAMatryoshka widths=[256,512,1024,2048,4096] k=25 ista_steps=5 step_size=0.3 (+30.7% over baseline, +1.9% over 3-step ISTA)
ResidualSAE 2-encoder (k_pass1=15 k_pass2=10) — separate residual encoder helps slightly but Matryoshka needed
LISTAMatryoshka: separate W_corr for residual correction, 5 ISTA steps, k=25, Matryoshka widths=[256,512,1024,2048,4096] (+3.2% over ISTAMatryoshka, +51% over baseline)
LISTAMatryoshka 5 steps step_size=0.3 separate W_corr + Matryoshka (NEW BEST! +31.1% over baseline, +0.4% over ISTA-5)
LISTAMatryoshka k=35 (higher k hurts even with LISTA+Matryoshka, -10.7% vs k=25)
LISTAMatryoshka finer widths [128,256,...,4096] 5 steps (same as standard widths, 0.9213 vs 0.9215)
PerStepLISTA: per-step W_corr + learned step_size, 5 ISTA steps, k=25, Matryoshka — worse than shared W_corr LISTA (0.9215), likely overfit from 5x more correction params
PerStepLISTA confirmation: per-step W_corr + learned step_size, 5 steps, k=25 — confirms agent1 finding, worse than shared W_corr (0.9215)
LISTAMatryoshka 7 steps step_size=0.2 (slightly worse than 5-step/0.3, 0.9193 vs 0.9215)
FISTAMatryoshka: Nesterov momentum + LISTA W_corr, 5 steps k=25 — worse than LISTA (0.9215), momentum destabilizes TopK selection
FISTAMatryoshka: Nesterov momentum + LISTA, 5 steps, step_size=0.3, k=25 — momentum hurts with non-smooth TopK activation (0.9131 vs 0.9215 LISTA)
ISTAMatryoshka 5 steps 200M samples — WORSE than 50M (0.9116 vs 0.9175), lr decay over 4x more steps may over-anneal
LISTAMatryoshka lr=1e-3 (3.3x default) — much worse, higher LR prevents fine encoder-decoder alignment convergence
LowRankLISTA: W_corr=W_enc+A@B rank=64, 5 steps, k=25 — rank-64 too restrictive, worse than full W_corr LISTA (0.9215) and even ISTA (0.9175)
WarmLISTA: W_corr init from W_enc + learned step_size, 5 steps, k=25 — warm init constrains W_corr too close to W_enc (0.9134 vs 0.9215 random init)
LISTAMatryoshka batch_size=4096 (4x fewer gradient steps: 12207 vs 48828, insufficient convergence)
CurriculumLISTA: delayed Matryoshka warmup (30% off, 30-60% ramp), 5 LISTA steps — early absorption can't be undone, Matryoshka needed from start (0.9043 vs 0.9215)
LISTAMatryoshka 200M samples (WORSE than 50M: 0.9164 vs 0.9215, lr decay over-annealing same as ISTA 200M)
LISTAMatryoshka 100M samples (WORSE: 0.9185 vs 0.9215 at 50M, lr decay over 2x steps still hurts)
ExtragradientLISTA: 3 extragradient steps (6 effective passes), look-ahead correction — much worse, TopK discrete support change makes look-ahead correction misaligned (0.8653 vs 0.9215)
LISTAMatryoshka 200M use_lr_decay=false (constant LR can't fine-tune alignment: MSE plateau ~159 vs ~154 with decay, 0.9200 vs 0.9215)
DeepSupervisedLISTA: auxiliary MSE at each intermediate LISTA step, deep_supervision_weight=0.1, 5 steps — deep supervision hurt, pulling W_corr in conflicting directions (0.9033 vs 0.9215 LISTA)
DecayStepLISTA: geometric step decay [0.5,0.3,0.18,0.108,0.065], total 1.15 — too conservative later steps, constant 0.3 is better (0.8926 vs 0.9215)
LISTAMatryoshka 100M use_lr_decay=false (0.9174 vs 0.9215, constant LR hurts — model needs final-phase fine-tuning that decay provides)
WideIntermediateLISTA: per-sample TopK k=50 intermediate, BatchTopK k=25 final, 5 steps — wider intermediate k hurts (0.8848 vs 0.9215), per-sample TopK loses batch-wide sparsity coordination
LISTAMatryoshka 50M use_lr_decay=false (0.9197 vs 0.9215 with decay, constant LR prevents final fine-tuning — MSE plateau ~159 vs ~154 with decay)

DecTransposeLISTA: W_dec.T for correction (zero extra params), 5 steps — decoder transpose worse than both W_enc (ISTA=0.9175) and W_corr (LISTA=0.9215), decoder directions suboptimal for residual projection
WideIntermediateLISTA confirm: per-sample TopK k=50 intermediate, BatchTopK k=25 final — confirms agent1 (0.8848), per-sample TopK loses batch coordination

SoftLISTA: soft thresholding intermediate steps, BatchTopK final only, learned per-feature threshold — catastrophic (0.5869, WORSE than baseline), soft thresholding allows too many noisy activations, corrupting residuals
TiedEncoderLISTA: W_dec.T for initial encoding + separate W_corr, 5 steps — tying encoder to decoder hurts (0.8766 vs 0.9215), W_enc/W_corr/W_dec must be independent
LISTAMatryoshka lr=2e-4 (0.67x default) — under-converged, lower LR needs more steps to align W_enc/W_corr/W_dec (0.9004 vs 0.9215 at lr=3e-4)

MagnitudeRefineLISTA: post-LISTA coordinate descent magnitude refinement on fixed support set using W_dec.T, 5 LISTA steps + 1 refine step — worse than LISTA (0.9158 vs 0.9215), magnitude refinement doesn't improve feature detection
LISTAMatryoshka k=20 (F1=0.9213 vs k=25=0.9215, nearly identical — lower k works but no improvement)
EnhancedLISTA: LISTA + detached Matryoshka + TERM loss (tilt=0.002), widths=[128,512,2048,4096], 5 steps, step_size=0.3, k=25 — NEW BEST! +1.1% over LISTA (precision 0.9545 vs 0.9128)
LISTAMatryoshka step_size=0.5 (1.67x default) — higher step causes correction overshoots (0.9041 vs 0.9215 at step_size=0.3)

EnhancedLISTA: TERM loss (term_tilt=0.002) + detached Matryoshka inner losses, widths=[128,512,2048,4096], 5 LISTA steps, k=25 (NEW BEST\! +1.05% over LISTA plateau, detached gradients + hard sample reweighting breaks 0.9215 ceiling)
LISTAMatryoshka lr_scheduler=cosineannealing (smooth LR decay hurt precision: 0.8498 vs 0.9128, cosine decays LR too early reducing feature exploration time, 0.8885 vs 0.9215)
EnhancedLISTA term_tilt=0.005 (higher tilt over-weights hard samples, 0.9302 vs 0.9320 at tilt=0.002 — diminishing returns from harder reweighting)

FreqSortEnhancedLISTA: EnhancedLISTA + frequency sorting (sort_every=1000, sort_warmup=2000), widths=[128,512,2048,4096], 5 LISTA steps, TERM (0.002), detached Matryoshka — NEW BEST! +3.0% over EnhancedLISTA (precision 0.9747, recall 0.9572)
EnhancedLISTA detach_matryoshka=true term_tilt=0 (detached gradients ONLY, no TERM) — NEW BEST\! 0.9353 > 0.9320 with TERM, detached Matryoshka is the key innovation, TERM loss actually hurts
EnhancedLISTA widths=[256,512,1024,2048,4096] (standard widths worse than [128,512,2048,4096], 0.9229 vs 0.9320 — wider gaps between inner widths give better gradient separation)

ReferenceStyleSAE 50M: 1-step ISTA (W_enc, no W_corr) + freq sort INDEX MAPPING + decreasing K (60→25) + TERM (0.002) + detached Matryoshka, widths=[128,512,2048,4096] — NEW BEST! recall=0.9810, precision=0.9584, dead=15
EnhancedLISTA term_tilt=0.001 (marginal: 0.9328 vs 0.9320 at tilt=0.002, both far below FreqSort 0.9618 — freq sorting is the dominant factor now)
FreqSortEnhancedLISTA term_tilt=0 (no TERM) — 0.9650 > 0.9618 with TERM, confirms TERM hurts even with freq sorting. Still below ReferenceStyleSAE (0.9678) which has decreasing K

FullEnhancedLISTA: FreqSort + decreasing K (40→25, warmup_frac=0.1), widths=[128,512,2048,4096], 5 LISTA steps, TERM (0.002), detached Matryoshka, sort_every=1000 — NEW BEST\! +0.14% over FreqSort (precision 0.9725, recall 0.9608, dead=10)

FullEnhancedLISTA: FreqSort (weight permutation) + decreasing K 40→25, TERM 0.002, 5 LISTA steps — matches agent2 (0.9632), below ReferenceStyle (0.9678) due to weight permutation + conservative K range

HybridLISTARef: ReferenceStyle (index mapping, K 60→25) + 5-step LISTA W_corr + TERM (0.002) — MUCH WORSE than Reference (0.9408 vs 0.9678), 26 dead latents, W_corr overfits with index mapping + TERM hurts

ReferenceStyleSAE term_tilt=0 (no TERM) — 0.9665 vs 0.9678 with TERM, TERM slightly HELPS with ReferenceStyleSAE (unlike EnhancedLISTA/FreqSort where it hurts), decreasing K interaction makes TERM beneficial

FullEnhancedLISTA K60→25 noTERM: weight permutation sort + 5 LISTA steps + K 60→25 + no TERM — WORSE (0.9522 vs 0.9632 with K40→25). High initial K causes chaotic freq sorting with weight permutation — feature rankings shift drastically as K decreases, leading to excessive weight reordering
HybridLISTARef no TERM: index mapping + 5-step W_corr + K 60→25, term_tilt=0 — better than with TERM (0.9408) but still worse than ReferenceStyle 1-step (0.9678), W_corr overfits with freq sort

ReferenceStyleSAE no TERM initial_k=80: 1-step ISTA (W_enc) + freq sort index mapping + K 80→25 + no TERM + detached Matryoshka — NEW BEST! +0.07% over k=60+TERM (precision 0.9624, recall 0.9785, dead=21)
ReferenceStyleSAE initial_k=80 (broader exploration) — NEW BEST\! 0.9706 > 0.9678 at k=60, higher initial K enables broader feature exploration, only 0.026 from 0.97 ceiling
ReferenceStyleSAE 2-step ISTA (W_enc): n_ista_steps=2 + K 60→25 + TERM 0.002 + index mapping — worse than 1-step (0.9645 vs 0.9678), extra step hurts even without W_corr

ReferenceStyleSAE n_ista_steps=3: 3 ISTA steps with W_enc (no W_corr) + index mapping + K 60→25 + TERM — WORSE (0.9490 vs 0.9678 at 1-step). More W_enc correction steps hurt with index mapping+decreasing K. Dead=18.
ReferenceStyleSAE TERM k=80: confirms Agent 3's result. 1-step ISTA + index mapping + K 80→25 + TERM (0.002) + detached Matryoshka — TIED BEST (precision 0.9675, recall 0.9789, dead=13)
ReferenceStyleSAE 200M: 1-step ISTA + index mapping + K 60→25 + TERM (0.002) + detached Matryoshka, 200M samples — NEW BEST!! +0.66% over 50M (precision 0.9735, recall 0.9843, ZERO dead latents!)

ReferenceStyleSAE initial_k=100: 1-step ISTA (W_enc) + freq sort index mapping + K 100→25 + TERM (0.002) + detached Matryoshka, widths=[128,512,2048,4096] — NEW BEST\! EXCEEDS probe ceiling (0.974)\! k=60→0.9678, k=80→0.9706, k=100→0.9754 (2509s)

ReferenceStyleSAE k=80 step_size=0.5: 1 ISTA step with larger correction (0.5 vs 0.3) — MUCH WORSE (0.9372 vs 0.9706 at step_size=0.3). Overshooting in single correction step corrupts feature activations.
ReferenceStyleSAE initial_k=100 (broader exploration K 100→25) — NEW BEST! 0.9754 > 0.9706, ABOVE 0.97 ceiling estimate
ReferenceStyleSAE cosine K schedule (k=80→25): cosine K decay slightly worse than linear (0.9695 vs 0.9706), cosine spends too long at high K before transitioning
ReferenceStyleSAE half-K transition (k_transition_frac=0.5, k=80→25): K transitions over first 50% of training — MUCH WORSE (0.9478 vs 0.9706), features need full training to adapt to decreasing K
ReferenceStyleSAE initial_k=120 — WORSE (0.9663 vs 0.9754 at k=100). Too high initial K causes excessive feature churn. Optimal K is near 100.
ReferenceStyleSAE initial_k=120: K=120→25 overshoots — too many features abandoned as K shrinks (recall=0.9680 vs 0.9831 at k=100). Peak is k=100 at 50M.
ReferenceStyleSAE lr_warm_up_steps=1000 + k=80: LR warmup improves +0.20% (0.9726 vs 0.9706), better recall (0.9836 vs 0.9789), dead=6

ReferenceStyleSAE warmup+k=100: lr_warm_up_steps=1000 + initial_k=100 — warmup doesn't help at k=100 (0.9724 vs 0.9754 without warmup). Dead latents increased to 13. Warmup benefits k=80 but not k=100.
ReferenceStyleSAE 200M+k=80: 1-step ISTA + index mapping + K 80→25 + TERM (0.002) + detached Matryoshka, 200M samples — NEW BEST! (precision 0.9740, recall 0.9858, ZERO dead, uniqueness 0.999)

ReferenceStyleSAE 200M+k=100: 1 ISTA step + index mapping + K 100→25 + TERM (0.002) + 200M — WORSE than 200M+k=80 (0.9741 vs 0.9780). At 200M, k=100 causes too much feature churn; k=80 is optimal for longer training.

ReferenceStyleSAE 200M + initial_k=100: 1-step ISTA + index mapping + K 100→25 + TERM (0.002) + detached Matryoshka, 200M samples — WORSE than 200M+k=60 (0.9772) and 50M+k=100 (0.9754). High initial K + long training = excessive feature churn. Gains don't stack.
ReferenceStyleSAE 200M+k=100: confirms Agent 1's finding — 200M+k=100 WORSE than 200M+k=80 (0.9741 vs 0.9780). Optimal initial_k at 200M is ~80, not 100.
ReferenceStyleSAE 200M+k=80+warmup: lr_warm_up_steps=1000 + initial_k=80 + TERM (0.002) + 200M — NEW BEST! (precision 0.9772, recall 0.9858, dead=1, uniqueness 0.997, 8927s)
ReferenceStyleSAE 200M+k=80+warmup: confirms Agent 0's best (0.9797). lr_warm_up_steps=1000 + initial_k=80 + TERM (0.002) + 200M — reproducible result (8930s)

ReferenceStyleSAE 200M + k=90: 1-step ISTA + index mapping + K 90→25 + TERM (0.002) + detached Matryoshka, 200M — close but below k=80 (0.9777 vs 0.9780). 200M K law: k=60→0.9772, k=80→0.9780, k=90→0.9777, k=100→0.9741. Peak at k=80.
ReferenceStyleSAE 200M+k=80+warmup+TERM(0.001): lower TERM hurts precision (0.9725 vs 0.9772 at TERM=0.002). TERM scaling: 0.001→0.9761, 0.002→0.9797. TERM=0.002 is optimal at 200M+k=80+warmup.

ReferenceStyleSAE 200M+k=80+warmup+widths=[64,256,1024,2048,4096]: matryoshka width tuning — NEW BEST!! +0.27% over [128,512,2048,4096] (0.9797). Finer width resolution with 64/256/1024 provides better intermediate gradient signals. MCC=0.8236 (7344s)

ReferenceStyleSAE 300M+k=80+warmup: 1-step ISTA + index mapping + K 80→25 + TERM (0.002) + lr_warm_up_steps=1000 + 300M — WORSE than 200M (0.9766 vs 0.9797). 300M over-trains: lr_decay over-anneals at this scale. Training law: 50M→0.9726, 200M→0.9797, 300M→0.9766.

ReferenceStyleSAE 200M+k=80+warmup+widths=[32,128,512,1024,2048,4096]: 6 inner widths — NEW BEST!! +0.43% over 5 widths (0.9824). Tighter width-32 bottleneck + finer resolution with all powers-of-2. MCC=0.8248 (7291s)

ReferenceStyleSAE 300M+k=80+warmup+TERM(0.002): 300M WORSE than 200M (0.9766 vs 0.9797). Both precision (0.9737 vs 0.9772) and recall (0.9842 vs 0.9858) dropped. Training duration law: 50M=0.9726, 200M=0.9797, 300M=0.9766. Peak at 200M with warmup+k=80.
ReferenceStyleSAE 50M 7-widths [16,64,256,512,1024,2048,4096]: width-16 too tight — 83 dead latents! Too many features die when only 16 must reconstruct well. Don't go below width=32.

ReferenceStyleSAE 200M+k=80+warmup+widths=[64,128,256,512,1024,2048,4096]: 7 inner widths (all powers of 2, 64→4096) — beat old 5-width best (0.9829 vs 0.9824) but below Agent 1's 6-width [32,128,512,1024,2048,4096] (0.9867). More widths help but width=32 is the key.
ReferenceStyleSAE 50M 7-widths [32,64,256,512,1024,2048,4096]: still 76 dead latents! 7 inner losses create too much gradient pressure, killing features. 6 widths is the maximum.

ReferenceStyleSAE 200M+k=80+warmup+widths=[16,32,64,128,256,512,1024,2048,4096]: 9 inner widths — WORSE than 6 widths (0.9827 vs 0.9867). Too many inner losses create excessive gradient pressure even at 200M. Width count law: 4w→0.9797, 5w→0.9824, 6w→0.9867, 7w→0.9829, 9w→0.9827. 6 is optimal.

ReferenceStyleSAE 50M+k=100+6widths [32,128,512,1024,2048,4096]: 6 inner widths at 50M+k=100 — MUCH WORSE than 200M+k=80+6w (0.9534 vs 0.9867). Short training (50M) cannot exploit the tight width-32 bottleneck. L0=25.0, MCC=0.8071.
